steps.title: Set up the mixture equation
steps.content:
Pure saline is conserved when mixing solutions. Identify the amount of pure saline in each component:
  • 4 liters at 12% contains $4 \times 0.12 = 0.48$ liters of pure saline
  • Let $x$ = liters of 28% solution to add, containing $0.28x$ liters of pure saline
  • Final mixture: $(4 + x)$ liters at 18% contains ${0.18}(4 + x)$ liters of pure saline

steps.title: Create the conservation equation
steps.content: Pure saline from both initial solutions must equal pure saline in the final mixture:

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Expand the right side:
$$0.48 + 0.28x = 0.72 + 0.18x$$
Subtract $0.18x$ from both sides and subtract $0.48$ from both sides:
$$0.10x = 0.24$$
Divide both sides by $0.10$:
$$x = 2.4$$
We must add 2.4 liters of the 28% saline solution.
